name: Smoking Salmon and Trout Part VIII - Making Lox
recipeInstructions:
1. Freshen mild-salted fish by soaking in several changes of fresh water.
2. Thin pieces will require less time than thick pieces that may take up to 24 hours. Test by tasting, remembering that the subsequent smoking will dry the fish and concentrate the saltiness.
3. Drain the freshened fish on the smoking racks.
4. Smoke at 85°F with medium-density smoke for 6-8 hours (forced draft) or 12-16 hours (natural draft).
5. Cool the fish before wrapping and freeze any surplus.
6. Nova Lox and Lox Salmon:
- Fish may be either fresh or frozen. Frozen is actually better as the freezing and thawing removes some of the moisture.
- If the fish are frozen whole, fillet them when they are half thawed.
- Cut into pieces according to thickness.
- Use the thick portions for Lox and the thin ones in kippering, drying, canning or eating fresh.
7. Make a dry salt-sugar mix of equal parts sugar and pickling salt.
8. Dry salt by placing the pieces in a container of mix. Cover each piece but do not rub it into the flesh.
9. Sprinkle some mix into a container and lay the salt mix dredged pieces on skin side down. Sprinkle each layer with more mix and add another layer, etc.
10. Times for salting according to thickness are the same as for Scotch smoked dry salting above.
11. Remove the pieces from the mix, rinse and drain.
12. Brine the pieces in a 90°F salinity of 2 1/2 cups salt per 2 quarts water (optional bay leaves).
13. Keep brine and fish cool throughout the process.
14. Freshen the fish under running water more or less to taste; the table is just an approximation.
Thickness Brining Time Freshening Time
3/4 inch 9 hours 45 minutes
1 inch 12 hours 1 hour 1-1/2 inches 18 hours
1-1/2 hours
2 inches 24 hours 2 hours
